steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
Subtracting the second polynomial means changing the sign of every term inside the second parentheses. This keeps unlike powers separate, so the $x^6$ term and the $x^3$ term stay in different groups.
$$(4x^3-5x+8)-(7x^6+9x-6)$$
$$=4x^3-5x+8-7x^6-9x+6$$

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content:
Now combine terms with the same power of $x$. The $x^6$ and $x^3$ terms do not combine, while the linear terms and constants do.
$$-7x^6+4x^3+(-5x-9x)+(8+6)$$
$$=-7x^6+4x^3-14x+14$$
The answer is B.
